item.title: The Pope’s Admirers Are Missing Something
item.description: Leo’s new encyclical challenges much more than Big Tech.
item.dc:creator: Randy Boyagoda

item.title: The Apple Car Is Finally Here
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: Condemning a Nazi Tattoo Shouldn’t Be This Hard
item.description: Why aren’t more Democrats calling out Graham Platner?
item.dc:creator: Mike Nelson

item.title: Trump Takes His Cut
item.description: Federal stakes in public companies may enrich the government, but they are bad for America.
item.dc:creator: James Surowiecki

item.title: Words of War
item.description: Four terms that are proving unhelpful in understanding the war with Iran
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: The Blue-State Delusion Over Unions
item.description: They don’t always work in the public interest.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bagley

item.title: John Cornyn Lost With His Boots Off
item.description: Like fellow Republicans exiled by the president, he still accepted Trump’s claim to inhabit the will of the party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The Pope Grasps the Limits of AI
item.description: The Vatican’s encyclical is actually about what technology can never do.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig

item.title: The Gas-Tax Reckoning
item.description: Pausing it would be a lose-lose proposition.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: The Advice I Hope You’ll Never Need
item.description: I survived two school shootings. This is what I learned.
item.dc:creator: Zoe Weissman
